title: Instagram Photography Masterclass
subtitle: Master the art of visual storytelling for social media
description: Learn the fundamental principles of composition, lighting, and editing to create stunning Instagram photos that engage your audience. This compact course takes you from smartphone snapshots to professional-quality posts in just 3 weeks.
targetAudience: Social media enthusiasts, aspiring content creators, and small business owners looking to improve their visual brand
objectives: Apply composition techniques to create visually compelling photographs
Analyze lighting conditions and adapt shooting strategies accordingly
Edit photos using industry-standard tools to enhance visual impact
Curate a cohesive Instagram feed that reflects personal or brand aesthetic
prerequisites: Access to a smartphone or camera

modules.title: Composition Fundamentals
modules.description: Master the core principles of visual composition that transform ordinary scenes into captivating images. Learn rule of thirds, leading lines, depth, and framing.
objectives: Apply the rule of thirds to improve visual balance
Identify and use leading lines to guide viewer attention
Create depth through layering and perspective

lessons.title: Rule of Thirds and Visual Balance
lessons.description: Understanding how to position subjects within the frame for maximum impact using the rule of thirds grid.
objectives: Apply the rule of thirds in real-world photography

lessons.title: Leading Lines and Depth
lessons.description: Learn how lines in your environment can draw viewers' eyes through your photos and create a sense of depth.
objectives: Identify and compose with leading lines

lessons.title: Framing and Subject Placement
lessons.description: Discover how to use your environment to frame subjects and create more dynamic compositions.
objectives: Create intentional frames within your photography

lessons.title: Composition Critique Workshop
lessons.description: Analyze real Instagram photos and discuss what makes them work compositionally.
objectives: Critically evaluate composition in professional work

quiz.question: When applying the rule of thirds, where should you typically place your subject for maximum visual impact?
options: Always in the exact center of the frame
At one of the four intersecting points or along the grid lines
In the bottom left corner of the frame
As far to the edge as possible
quiz.explanation: The rule of thirds divides your frame into 9 equal sections. Placing subjects at the intersecting points creates more dynamic and visually appealing compositions than centering them.

quiz.question: What is a leading line in photography?
options: A straight line that must be perfectly horizontal
Any element in your photo that naturally guides the viewer's eye through the composition
A line drawn in post-processing to mark the rule of thirds
Only lines created by roads or architecture
quiz.explanation: Leading lines can be any element—roads, rivers, shadows, or even eye gaze—that naturally draws the viewer's attention through the frame toward your main subject.

quiz.question: How can you create depth in a photograph without using a professional camera?
options: You cannot create depth with a smartphone
By layering elements at different distances: foreground, middle ground, and background
By using only black and white filters
Depth is automatically created when you zoom in
quiz.explanation: Depth perception is created when viewers can see distinct layers in your photo. Including interesting elements in the foreground, middle, and background creates this layering effect.

modules.title: Lighting and Exposure Mastery
modules.description: Transform your photography by understanding how light works. Learn to work with natural light, golden hour, backlighting, and exposure compensation.
objectives: Evaluate ambient light conditions and adjust shooting strategies
Utilize golden hour and blue hour for optimal natural lighting
Control exposure using smartphone and camera settings

lessons.title: Understanding Natural Light
lessons.description: Explore how natural light changes throughout the day and how to use it to your advantage in photography.
objectives: Identify optimal times for natural light photography

lessons.title: Golden Hour and Blue Hour Photography
lessons.description: Master the most flattering times of day for photography and learn why these hours produce magic in your images.
objectives: Plan photoshoots around optimal lighting times

lessons.title: Backlighting and Creative Shadows
lessons.description: Learn to use backlighting to create silhouettes, rim light effects, and atmospheric images.
objectives: Create professional backlighting effects

lessons.title: Exposure Compensation Techniques
lessons.description: Master exposure settings on your phone or camera to properly expose your subjects in challenging lighting.
objectives: Adjust exposure for properly lit photos in any condition

quiz.question: What is the 'golden hour' in photography?
options: The first hour after sunrise or the last hour before sunset when light is warm and soft
The hour when the sun is directly overhead
Any hour during midday when the sun is brightest
A filter setting on Instagram
quiz.explanation: The golden hour occurs shortly after sunrise or before sunset when the sun is at a low angle, creating warm, soft, flattering light that's ideal for most types of photography.

quiz.question: When should you use backlighting in your photography?
options: Never, because it makes your subject too dark
When you want to create silhouettes or add a glowing rim light around your subject
Only when you have professional lighting equipment
Never, because it causes lens flare
quiz.explanation: Backlighting, when the light source is behind your subject, creates beautiful effects like silhouettes, rim lighting, and separation from the background.

modules.title: Editing and Instagram Aesthetics
modules.description: Take your photos to the next level with professional editing techniques. Learn color grading, preset creation, and how to build a cohesive Instagram aesthetic.
objectives: Edit photos using Lightroom or Snapseed to enhance visual appeal
Create a consistent color palette and visual style
Develop preset strategies for efficient batch editing

lessons.title: Editing Fundamentals with Lightroom Mobile
lessons.description: Learn the essential editing adjustments: exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows, clarity, and saturation.
objectives: Apply professional editing adjustments to raw photos

lessons.title: Color Grading for Brand Aesthetics
lessons.description: Master color grading techniques to create a signature look that defines your Instagram aesthetic.
objectives: Apply consistent color grading to create visual cohesion

lessons.title: Creating and Using Editing Presets
lessons.description: Build custom presets to speed up your editing workflow while maintaining a consistent aesthetic across your feed.
objectives: Develop custom presets for efficient editing

lessons.title: Curating Your Instagram Feed
lessons.description: Learn how to plan your Instagram grid layout, maintain visual consistency, and create an aesthetically pleasing feed.
objectives: Design a cohesive Instagram feed layout

quiz.question: What is the primary benefit of using editing presets?
options: They automatically edit your photos perfectly without any adjustments
They provide a starting point for consistent editing that can be customized per photo
They replace the need for learning manual editing techniques
They can only be used on iPhone
quiz.explanation: Presets save your editing settings as templates, allowing you to apply a consistent look across multiple photos while still maintaining the flexibility to adjust each image individually.

quiz.question: How can you create a cohesive Instagram aesthetic?
options: Post random photos with different editing styles
Use the same preset without any customization on every photo
Maintain a consistent color palette, lighting style, and editing approach across your feed
Only post photos taken at the same location
quiz.explanation: A cohesive aesthetic comes from consistent choices in color grading, editing style, subject matter, and composition—not from mindlessly applying the same preset to every photo.

quiz.question: Which editing adjustment should you typically apply first when editing a photo?
options: Saturation, to make colors pop immediately
Clarity, to add detail to your image
Exposure, to properly light your photo before making other adjustments
Vibrance, to enhance color intensity
quiz.explanation: Exposure should be adjusted first because it serves as the foundation for all other edits. Once your photo is properly exposed, you can then adjust contrast, color, and other elements.

bonusResources.title: Instagram Growth Checklist
bonusResources.description: A practical checklist for optimizing your Instagram profile and posting strategy

bonusResources.title: Free Editing Presets Bundle
bonusResources.description: 5 professional editing presets compatible with Lightroom Mobile and Snapseed

bonusResources.title: Photography Inspiration Boards
bonusResources.description: Organized inspiration boards by photography style and aesthetic
